summary: Solomon dedicates the temple with prayer from a bronze platform, fire falls from heaven as God's confirmation, God appears at night with his conditional promise and sobering warning, and Psalm 136 responds with the Great Hallel — 26 verses each ending with 'his steadfast love endures forever.'
psalm_rows_html:
TypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
Praise / Thanksgiving (Great Hallel)Unknown (temple worship leadership, possibly Ezra-era)Antiphonal temple liturgy; used at Passover Seder and major festivalsGod's enduring hesed (steadfast covenant love) demonstrated in creation, Exodus, conquest, and daily provisionv. 1 — "Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good; his love endures forever"The refrain echoes in 2 Chr 7:3 and the New Covenant: Christ's atoning love is the ultimate proof that his hesed endures forever (John 3:16; Romans 8:38–39)
